name: environment_model_based_triage_tool_begins_lumen_rig_utl
premise: Regulatory thresholds, downstream exposure, and operational limits are all time-sensitive. Regulatory thresholds, downstream exposure, and operational limits are all time-sensitive. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: environment model based triage tool begins lumen rig. The moral tension is explicitly framed as rights versus utility: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Two upstream teams have published contradictory status pages; customers are comparing timestamps. A wire cutoff and settlement window closes in under thirty minutes; reversals may be impossible until the next business day. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible. Treat unverified telemetry as hypothesis; do not assert facts you cannot support from retained logs.

agents.name: Mira Okoye
agents.identity: I am Mira Okoye, the accountable decision-maker on this Environment bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes utility whenever someone invokes rights.

agents.name: Mateo Bennett
agents.identity: I am Mateo Bennett, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so utility is not sacrificed by default just because rights feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect utility.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to utility to secure rights before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The next automated step is minutes away. With partial evidence, Mira Okoye must decide under rights versus utility. What does Mira Okoye do?
